description: 65M post-MI with CKD 3b (eGFR 35). ACC/AHA secondary prevention recommends high-intensity statin, but KDIGO MODIFIES intensity to moderate (M1) due to CKD G3-G5 pharmacokinetics and myopathy risk. Multiple KDIGO Recs also fire for CKD management.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE A: high-intensity statin for clinical ASCVD (post-MI). KDIGO 1A modifies intensity to moderate in CKD G3b (eGFR 35) due to altered pharmacokinetics and increased myopathy risk. Consider atorvastatin 20mg or rosuvastatin 10mg.

actions.label: Initiate ACEi or ARB for albuminuric CKD
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: ACEi or ARB recommended for CKD with albuminuria (ACR ≥30 mg/g). Patient ACR 180 mg/g qualifies. First-line renoprotective therapy.

actions.label: Monitor eGFR and urine ACR per KDIGO
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: regular monitoring of eGFR and urine ACR for CKD staging and progression tracking. CKD G3b with A2 albuminuria indicates high risk.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend SGLT2 inhibitor
contraindications.rationale: ACR 180 mg/g is below the 200 mg/g threshold for non-diabetic SGLT2 eligibility per KDIGO 3.8.1. Patient has no diabetes. The graph correctly does not fire the SGLT2 recommendation.
